blocks.text: Vindicate me, O God, and defend my cause against an ungodly people; from those who are deceitful and unjust deliver me!

Ps 42–43: Longing to join the community in the Temple. This is the first psalm in the “Elohistic Psalter” (see Introduction). These two chapters form one poem as indicated by the identical refrain in 42.6,11 and 43.5, and petition to be in the Temple (like Pss 27; 63; 84). Here the sufferer, away from the Temple, complains of taunts by unbelievers (42.9–10) and pleads for a guide for the journey home. Distinctive is the psalmist’s alternation between discouragement and resolve, despair and hope.
For you are the God in whom I take refuge; why have you cast me off? Why must I walk about mournfully because of the oppression of the enemy?
O send out your light and your truth; let them lead me; let them bring me to your holy hill and to your dwelling.
Then I will go to the altar of God, to God my exceeding joy; and I will praise you with the harp, O God, my God.
Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why are you disquieted within me? Hope in God; for I shall again praise him, my help and my God.
43.1–5 : The tone changes and the prayer becomes more robust, asking God to send , qualities personified as heavenly beings able to lead the psalmist to God’s shrine.
